items.title: Debug a failing pytest with one click
items.scenario: `test_chunker_is_lossless` fails on one input out of 100.
items.explanation: Set a gutter breakpoint inside the test. Use the 'Pytest current file' launch config. Pause at the breakpoint, inspect `text`, `chunks`, `len`. Two minutes to root cause.

items.title: Attach to a notebook kernel
items.scenario: A Jupyter cell hangs on a long LLM call; you want to know what it's sending.
items.explanation: Set a breakpoint in the cell. Click 'Debug Cell'. VS Code attaches to the kernel, pauses at the breakpoint, lets you inspect `messages` live. Disconnect when done — kernel keeps running.

items.title: Conditional + logpoint in a hot loop
items.scenario: Pipeline processes 10,000 docs; only `doc-7421` triggers the bug.
items.explanation: Right-click the gutter → Add Conditional Breakpoint → `doc.id == 'doc-7421'`. Add a logpoint on the line before: `'doc {doc.id} entering reranker'`. No code edits, no rebuild.

items.title: Remote-attach to a hanging pod
items.scenario: Production pod stalls intermittently; you can't reproduce locally.
items.explanation: Bake `debugpy.listen(('0.0.0.0', 5678))` into the entrypoint, guarded by an env var. Port-forward via `kubectl`. Attach from VS Code. Step through the exact production state.

items.title: Exception breakpoint on `RateLimitError`
items.scenario: You want to pause every time the LLM client raises `RateLimitError`, anywhere in the codebase.
items.explanation: VS Code → Run and Debug → Breakpoints panel → 'Add Function Breakpoint' / 'Add Exception Breakpoint' on `openai.RateLimitError`. Every occurrence pauses; no `breakpoint()` calls needed.
